name: India
summary: Day 71 Saturday (today markets closed). **INDIA TAKING IT WHILE GLOBAL EQUITIES MAKE RECORDS — Nifty 24,176.15 (-0.6%); Sensex 77,328.19 (-0.7%); Bank Nifty 55,310.55 (-1.3% LEADING THE DECLINE); USD/INR ₹94.47.** India is in the WORST position of the major economies for this crisis: import-dependent, FII-dependent, entering Q4 FY26 earnings season, currency under pressure, oil at $101. **No 2026 India-Iran high-tier engagement is confirmed today.** Indian government MEA fresh statement on Iran/Hormuz May 8-9: NONE surfaced (CORRECTIONS_PENDING.md retracts the prior brief's year-stale Araghchi-Jaishankar 20th JCM item — that meeting actually took place in May 2025). The economic-vs-diplomatic split therefore narrows: Indian media coverage of this cycle is now almost entirely market-impact-driven, with the diplomatic/strategic-autonomy frame effectively dormant in absence of confirmed engagement. **Causal chain (~65% crisis):** Brent at $101 → India imports ~85% of crude → CAD widens → INR ₹94.47 (vs pre-crisis ₹87.92, -7.5% to date); 1M -2.4% confirms persistent pressure → India 10Y G-Sec ~7.0%, +6-8 bps cycle as oil inflation intensifies (TradingEconomics). **FII outflows: ₹340.89 cr May 8 net vs DII +₹441 cr; ₹6,961.75 cr month outflows; ₹1.92 LAKH CR YTD 2026 — ALREADY EXCEEDED FULL-YEAR 2025 (₹1.66 LAKH CR).** FPI ownership in Indian equities at ~16%, lowest in nearly two decades. DII absorption (~₹1.7 lakh cr YTD via SIP) covering ~90% of FII selling but the gap is widening. Bank Nifty -1.3% leading the decline = financials pricing both NIM compression and FII channel withdrawal. Goodreturns: 'renewed Iran tension drives profit booking and FII outflows.' **Falsification:** Brent below $90 sustained for 10 sessions = INR back toward 92-93, FII flows positive = Nifty back to 25,000+. **If FII outflows accelerate past ₹2.5 lakh cr by end-Q2, the DII channel cracks.** Eight-million-strong Indian Gulf diaspora exposure factor remains active — UAE airspace closure through May 11; PRIOR FRAMING (preserved): PM Modi's May 5 Fujairah condemnation (without naming Iran — strategic-autonomy hedge); MEA 'unacceptable' statement on 3 Indian nationals injured at Fujairah; Chabahar OFAC waiver expired Apr 26.

current.deployment_context: India in the WORST position of the major economies for this crisis. FII -₹340.89 cr May 8; ₹1.92 lakh cr YTD ALREADY exceeds full-year 2025 (₹1.66 lakh cr). DII absorbing ~90% but gap widening. Bank Nifty -1.3% = financials pricing NIM compression + FII withdrawal. AI flows continue to bypass India IT services (Apple-Samsung-Intel halo to Korea/Taiwan/US). Falsification: Brent <$90 sustained 10 sessions = INR ~92-93, FII positive, Nifty 25,000+. If FII outflows accelerate past ₹2.5 lakh cr by end-Q2, DII channel cracks.

active_triggers.condition: Rupee crosses ₹95/USD — DEACTIVATED on ceasefire. Current ₹92.55. WATCHING.
India announces fuel-price pass-through OR Iran-rupee oil track restart via Chabahar within 30 days (by 2026-05-27)

FII outflow May 5 ₹3,621 cr; week net ₹13,771 cr selling vs DII ₹11,585 cr buying (absorption 84%, lower than prior ~90%).
